The **Colombia national under-20 football team** represents [Colombia](/wiki/Colombia "Colombia") in international under-20 [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") competitions and is overseen by the [Colombian Football Federation](/wiki/Colombian_Football_Federation "Colombian Football Federation").

Colombia has qualified for the [FIFA U-20 World Cup](/wiki/FIFA_U-20_World_Cup "FIFA U-20 World Cup") 11 times, and their standout performance came at the [2003 edition](/wiki/2003_FIFA_World_Youth_Championship "2003 FIFA World Youth Championship") where the team finished in third place and were rewarded the fair play award. For the [2011 FIFA U-20 World Cup](/wiki/2011_FIFA_U-20_World_Cup "2011 FIFA U-20 World Cup"), Colombia qualified automatically as hosts and reached the quarter-finals. Colombia have won the [South American Youth Championship](/wiki/South_American_Youth_Football_Championship "South American Youth Football Championship") three times: [1987](/wiki/1987_South_American_Youth_Championship "1987 South American Youth Championship"), [2005](/wiki/2005_South_American_Youth_Championship "2005 South American Youth Championship") and [2013](/wiki/2013_South_American_Youth_Championship "2013 South American Youth Championship"). The team also participates in the [Toulon Tournament](/wiki/Toulon_Tournament "Toulon Tournament"), of which Colombia is a three-time winner: 1999, 2000 and [2011](/wiki/2011_Toulon_Tournament "2011 Toulon Tournament"). Also, the team participates in the [Central American and Caribbean Games](/wiki/Football_at_the_Central_American_and_Caribbean_Games "Football at the Central American and Caribbean Games") and is a two-time winner: [2006](/wiki/Football_at_the_2006_Central_American_and_Caribbean_Games "Football at the 2006 Central American and Caribbean Games") and [2018](/wiki/Football_at_the_2018_Central_American_and_Caribbean_Games "Football at the 2018 Central American and Caribbean Games").

## Honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Colombia_national_under-20_football_team&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Honours")]

* **[FIFA U-20 World Cup](/wiki/FIFA_U-20_World_Cup "FIFA U-20 World Cup"):**
  + Third place (2): [2003](/wiki/2003_FIFA_U-20_World_Cup "2003 FIFA U-20 World Cup"), [2025](/wiki/2025_FIFA_U-20_World_Cup "2025 FIFA U-20 World Cup")
* **[CONMEBOL Sub 20](/wiki/CONMEBOL_Sub_20 "CONMEBOL Sub 20"):**
  + **Winners (3):** [1987](/wiki/1987_South_American_Youth_Championship "1987 South American Youth Championship"), [2005](/wiki/2005_South_American_Youth_Championship "2005 South American Youth Championship"), [2013](/wiki/2013_South_American_Youth_Championship "2013 South American Youth Championship")
  + **Runners-up (2):** [1988](/wiki/1988_South_American_Youth_Championship "1988 South American Youth Championship"), [2015](/wiki/2015_South_American_Youth_Championship "2015 South American Youth Championship")
  + Third place (5): [1964](/wiki/1964_South_American_Youth_Championship "1964 South American Youth Championship"), [1985](/wiki/1985_South_American_Youth_Championship "1985 South American Youth Championship"), [1992](/wiki/1992_South_American_Youth_Championship "1992 South American Youth Championship"), [2023](/wiki/2023_South_American_U-20_Championship "2023 South American U-20 Championship"), [2025](/wiki/2025_South_American_U-20_Championship "2025 South American U-20 Championship")
  + Fourth place (2): [2003](/wiki/2003_South_American_Youth_Championship "2003 South American Youth Championship"), [2019](/wiki/2019_South_American_U-20_Championship "2019 South American U-20 Championship")
* **[Toulon Tournament](/wiki/Toulon_Tournament "Toulon Tournament"):**
  + **Winners (3):** 1999, 2000, [2011](/wiki/2011_Toulon_Tournament "2011 Toulon Tournament")
  + **Runners-up (2)** 2001, [2013](/wiki/2013_Toulon_Tournament "2013 Toulon Tournament")
  + Fourth place (1): [2022](/wiki/2022_Maurice_Revello_Tournament "2022 Maurice Revello Tournament")
* **[Bolivarian Games](/wiki/Football_at_the_Bolivarian_Games "Football at the Bolivarian Games")**
  + **Runners-up (1):** [1985 Ecuador](/wiki/1985_Bolivarian_Games "1985 Bolivarian Games")
* **[Central American and Caribbean Games](/wiki/Football_at_the_Central_American_and_Caribbean_Games "Football at the Central American and Caribbean Games")**
  + **Winners (2):** [2006 Cartagena](/wiki/Football_at_the_2006_Central_American_and_Caribbean_Games "Football at the 2006 Central American and Caribbean Games"), [2018 Barranquilla](/wiki/Football_at_the_2018_Central_American_and_Caribbean_Games "Football at the 2018 Central American and Caribbean Games")
* **[Pan American Games](/wiki/Football_at_the_Pan_American_Games "Football at the Pan American Games")**
  + Fourth place (1): [2003 Santo Domingo](/wiki/Football_at_the_2003_Pan_American_Games_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_tournament "Football at the 2003 Pan American Games – Men's tournament")
* **[South American Games](/wiki/Football_at_the_South_American_Games "Football at the South American Games")**
  + Third place (3): 1990 Lima, [2018 Cochabamba](/wiki/Football_at_the_2018_South_American_Games "Football at the 2018 South American Games"), [2022 Asunción](/wiki/Football_at_the_2022_South_American_Games "Football at the 2022 South American Games")
